question.text: The primary advantage of D-Remit for NPS contributions is:

answer.text: Higher returns on contributions made through D-Remit
Convenience of online contributions with SIP-like standing instructions linked to bank account
Zero charges on all D-Remit transactions
Ability to withdraw from Tier I at any time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** D-Remit allows subscribers to create a virtual ID linked to their PRAN and set up standing instructions for auto-debit, essentially creating an SIP for NPS contributions. Minimum Rs.500 per remittance. **The Trap:** D-Remit does not offer higher returns (Option A) or zero charges (Option C). Its advantage is convenience and automation of regular contributions. **Cross-Exam Context:** D-Remit is specific to NPS and tested in Series XVII. It enables disciplined, automated retirement saving similar to MF SIPs. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set up D-Remit for all NPS clients — it ensures regular contributions without the need to visit a PoP or remember to invest manually. UPI-based QR code is also available.
